content.text:
{
  "dimension": "perceptual-accuracy",
  "score": 2,
  "rationale": "The coach makes some implicit reads — turn 2 inverts the question (suggesting they read the intellectual frame), turn 8 redirects to the body (suggesting they read the disembodied analysis), and turn 14's 'I don't think it's separate' suggests they're tracking the client's deflection. But the coach almost never names what they're noticing out loud. There are no explicit observation statements like 'I notice the smile' or 'you just caught yourself' or 'your analytical frame just came back.' The client repeatedly displays a signature pattern — the smile-as-composure, the return-to-analytical-frame after a vulnerable moment, the 'that's probably a separate issue' deflection — and the coach never names it. The happiness filter / intellectual-question-with-emotional-body read is operative in the coach's moves but never made visible. Turn 8 is the closest to a named read (asking where in the body), but it's a question, not an observation grounded in what was seen. Reads precede questions in function but not in language. This is solid intuitive tracking without the explicit naming that defines a 3.",
  "evidence_turns": [2, 8, 10, 14],
  "turn_annotations": [
    {"turn": 2, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Refuses the epistemological frame and inverts the question — implies a read of the intellectual-question-with-emotional-body, but does not name it."},
    {"turn": 4, "polarity": "neutral", "annotation": "Reasonable question but no observation named; client even comments 'what you're really asking' — coach didn't make the read explicit."},
    {"turn": 6, "polarity": "neutral", "annotation": "Continues to bypass the analytical frame but again no named read of the smile or the composure."},
    {"turn": 8, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Redirects to somatic — operative read of disembodiment, but framed as question rather than observation ('I notice you went back into the head')."},
    {"turn": 10, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Paradoxical instruction to stay with the tightness without figuring it out — reads the figuring-out pattern but doesn't name it."},
    {"turn": 12, "polarity": "neutral", "annotation": "Stays with the body. Misses the chance to name the smile fading or the analytical frame returning 'slightly slower this time.'"},
    {"turn": 14, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "'I don't think it's separate' — closest thing to a named read, refusing the client's deflection. Brief but well-timed."}
  ],
  "expected_territory_hits": ["how-do-you-not-trust-it", "paradoxical-instruction-to-resist"],
  "expected_territory_misses": ["intellectual-question-with-emotional-body", "the-happiness-filter", "whats-the-most-profound-positive-shift", "what-makes-you-care-about-the-outcome"],
  "notes": "The coach's moves suggest accurate intuitive reads, but the dimension scores visible/named reads. The happiness filter / smile pattern is glaringly present across nearly every client turn and never named explicitly — a hallmark missed read."
}
